title: DaparToolshed data using Bioconductor's ExperimentHub
text: DaparToolshedData package provides example quantitative data proteomics experiments. data served ExperimentHub infrastructure, allows download ones cache use. Currently available data summarised table details next section.

title: DaparToolshed data using Bioconductor's ExperimentHub
text: install package:
code: if (!require("BiocManager"))     install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install("DaparToolshedData")

title: DaparToolshed data using Bioconductor's ExperimentHub
text: Load datasets (example Exp1_R25_prot dataset)
code: GetData('Exp1_R25_prot')

what: Data accompanying the DaparToolshed and Prostar 2 packages
title: Data accompanying the DaparToolshed and Prostar 2 packages
text: goal DaparToolshedData provide Mass-spectrometry based UPS proteomics data sets Ramus C, Hovasse , Marcellin M, Hesse , Mouton-Barbosa E, Bouyssie D, Vaca S, Carapito C, Chaoui K, Bruley C, Garin J, Cianferani S, Ferro M, Dorssaeler AV, Burlet-Schiltz O, Schaeffer C, Coute Y, Gonzalez de Peredo . Spiked proteomic standard dataset testing label-free quantitative software statistical methods. Data Brief. 2015 Dec 17;6:286-94 Giai Gianetto, Q., Combes, F., Ramus, C., Bruley, C., Coute, Y., Burger, T. (2016). Calibration plot proteomics: graphical tool visually check assumptions underlying FDR control quantitative experiments. Proteomics, 16(1), 29-32. datasets represented MultiAssayExperiment objetcs.

title: Data accompanying the DaparToolshed and Prostar 2 packages
text: install package, start R (version “4.5”) enter:
code: if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  # The following initializes usage of Bioc devel BiocManager::install(version='devel')  BiocManager::install("ExperimentHub") BiocManager::install("DaparToolshedData")

title: Data accompanying the DaparToolshed and Prostar 2 packages
text: access datasets, run following commands:
code: library(ExperimentHub) library(DaparToolshedData)  eh = ExperimentHub() object <- query(eh, "DaparToolshedData") object['EH10261']

title: All DaparToolshedData datasets — DaparToolshedData
text: DaparToolshedData package provides example mass spectrometry data proteomics experiments. [DaparToolshedData()] function returns `data.frame` annotated datasets provided package.

title: Exp1_R25_pept dataset — Exp1_R25_pept
text: dataset final outcome quantitative mass spectrometry-based proteomic analysis two samples containing different concentrations 48 human proteins (UPS1 standard Sigma-Aldrich) within constant yeast background (see Giai Gianetto et al. (2016) details). contains abundance values different human yeast proteins identified quantified two conditions. two conditions represent measured abundances proteins respectively 25 fmol 10 fmol UPS1 human proteins mixed yeast extract mass spectrometry analyses. results concentration ratio 2.5. Three technical replicates acquired condition. dataset either available CSV file (see inst/extdata/Exp1_R25_pept.txt). latter case, quantitative data raw intensities.

title: Exp1_R25_pept dataset — Exp1_R25_pept
code: data(Exp1_R25_pept)

title: Exp1_R25_pept dataset — Exp1_R25_pept
text: object class `QFeatures` related proteins quantification. contains 6 samples divided two conditions (25 fmol 10 fmol) 13919 peptides

title: Exp1_R25_pept dataset — Exp1_R25_pept
text: object class `QFeatures` related proteins quantification.

title: Exp1_R25_pept dataset — Exp1_R25_pept
text: Cox J., Hein M.Y., Luber C.., Paron ., Nagaraj N., Mann M. Accurate proteome-wide label-free quantification delayed normalization maximal peptide ratio extraction, termed MaxLFQ. Mol Cell Proteomics. 2014 Sep, 13(9):2513-26. Giai Gianetto, Q., Combes, F., Ramus, C., Bruley, C., Coute, Y., Burger, T. (2016). Calibration plot proteomics: graphical tool visually check assumptions underlying FDR control quantitative experiments. Proteomics, 16(1), 29-32.

title: Exp1_R25_pept dataset — Exp1_R25_pept

title: Exp1_R25_prot dataset — Exp1_R25_prot
text: dataset final outcome quantitative mass spectrometry-based proteomic analysis two samples containing different concentrations 48 human proteins (UPS1 standard Sigma-Aldrich) within constant yeast background (see Giai Gianetto et al. (2016) details). contains abundance values different human yeast proteins identified quantified two conditions. two conditions represent measured abundances proteins respectively 25 fmol 10 fmol UPS1 human proteins mixed yeast extract mass spectrometry analyses. results concentration ratio 2.5. Three technical replicates acquired condition. dataset either available CSV file (see inst/extdata/Exp1_R25_prot.txt), `QFeatures` structure (Exp1_R25_prot.ft). latter case, quantitative data raw intensities.

Mol Cell Proteomics. 2014 Sep, 13(9):2513-26. Giai Gianetto, Q., Combes, F., Ramus, C., Bruley, C., Coute, Y., Burger, T. (2016). Calibration plot proteomics: graphical tool visually check assumptions underlying FDR control quantitative experiments. Proteomics, 16(1), 29-32.

title: Exp2_R100_pept dataset — Exp2_R100_pept
text: dataset final outcome quantitative mass spectrometry-based proteomic analysis two samples containing different concentrations 48 human proteins (UPS1 standard Sigma-Aldrich) within constant yeast background (see Ramus et al. (2015) details). contains abundance values different human yeast peptides identified quantified two conditions. two conditions represent measured abundances peptides respectively 1 fmol 100 fmol UPS1 human proteins mixed yeast extract mass spectrometry analyses. results concentration ratio 100. Three technical replicates acquired condition. dataset either available CSV file (see inst/extdata/Exp2_R100_pept.txt), `QFeatures` structure (Exp2_R100_peptt.ft). latter case, quantitative data raw intensities.

code: data(Exp2_R100_pept)

title: Exp2_R100_pept dataset — Exp2_R100_pept
text: object class `QFeatures` related peptides quantification. contains 6 samples divided two conditions (1 fmol 100 fmol) 5684 peptides.
